title: Diplacrum capitatum
taxonomy: Diplacrum capitatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. It belongs to the genus Diplacrum, which is a group of plants native to tropical regions. Diplacrum capitatum is a type of sedge that typically grows in wet environments, such as swamps or marshes. This plant is likely to be found in regions with high humidity and warm temperatures. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Liliopsida, class Liliopsida, and order Poales.
